title: overlay.go WatchResize: defer unlock + panic recovery
description:
File: internal/session/client/overlay.go, lines 163-173

WatchResize() runs as a bare goroutine (go c.WatchResize(sigCh), line 224). It handles SIGWINCH signals by resizing the VT and re-rendering. The VT mutex is locked/unlocked manually per signal.

Current risk: If Resize(), RenderScreen(), or RenderBar() panics, the goroutine dies, the mutex stays locked (deadlocking the session), and future terminal resizes are not handled. No recover, not relaunched.

Fix:
1. Convert manual vt.Mu.Unlock() to defer-based unlock (wrap in anonymous func).
2. Add a recover() inside the for-range loop body so a panic on one resize doesn't kill the handler — it should log the panic and continue handling future SIGWINCH signals.
